title: aciphylla procumbens
intro: Aciphylla procumbens, also known as New Zealand astelia or carrot fern, is a type of flowering plant in the asparagus family native to New Zealand. It has been traditionally used in Maori medicine, exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant's leaves and roots are used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, offering health benefits such as reduced inflammation and improved immune function. In herbal preparations, Aciphylla procumbens is often used in teas, tinctures, and infusions, although more research is needed to confirm its full range of medicinal properties.
